g.text: ## Identifier Scanning Pipeline: Characters → FSM → Keyword Lookup → Token

div.p: Critical Invariant: KEYWORDS.get() is called only after the while loop exits. The complete lexeme string is the lookup key — never a prefix.

text.tspan: → complete identifier string (O(k) slice, no concatenation)

div.p: Token( type = token_type, ← KEYWORD or IDENTIFIER lexeme = source[start:current], ← full word line = token_start_line, ← first char's line column = token_start_column ← first char's col )
Input: if FSM consumes: i, f peek() → '\0' or non-identifier → loop exits text = "if" KEYWORDS.get("if") → found → KEYWORD → Token(KEYWORD, "if", …)
Input: iffy FSM consumes: i, f, f, y peek() → non-identifier → loop exits text = "iffy" ← full word, not "if" KEYWORDS.get("iffy") → miss → default IDENTIFIER → Token(IDENTIFIER, "iffy", …)

Input: returning FSM consumes: r,e,t,u,r,n,i,n,g peek() → non-identifier → loop exits text = "returning" ← not "return" KEYWORDS.get("returning") → miss → default IDENTIFIER → Token(IDENTIFIER, "returning", …)

Why prefix matching is WRONG: Checking KEYWORDS inside the loop at each character would match "if" inside "iffy" and emit KEYWORD("if") + IDENTIFIER("fy") — incorrect. The while loop MUST complete before any lookup. This is enforced by calling KEYWORDS.get() only after the loop exits, using the complete text slice.
